_PURPOSE: This file is a self-contained semantic map of the entire project. Any AI model reading only this file should be able to fully understand the application's purpose, architecture, data models, component responsibilities, user flows, key constraints, and relationships — without reading any source file.

project.name: Amount Distribution Simulator
project.description: A React SPA (Single Page Application) that lets users assign monetary amounts to specific numbered boxes (1–100), then automatically generates N randomized 'folders' where each folder distributes the total assigned amount across a random selection of boxes. Outputs can be exported as TXT or PDF reports.

stack.styling: Vanilla CSS with custom properties (no TailwindCSS, no CSS-in-JS)

commands.dev: npm run dev  →  starts Vite dev server (default port 5174)
commands.build: npm run build  →  outputs optimized bundle to /dist
commands.preview: npm run preview  →  preview production build locally
commands.lint: npm run lint  →  ESLint with react-hooks + react-refresh plugins

core_concepts.BOX: One of 100 numbered slots (1–100). Each box can hold an assigned amount (₹). Visualized in a 10×10 grid.
core_concepts.BATCH: A user-created grouping: one amount value assigned to one or more box numbers. Multiple batches can coexist. Each batch has a unique numeric ID (Date.now()). Batches are the source of truth for what goes into a folder.
core_concepts.FOLDER: A generated distribution result. Each folder is a map of { boxNumber: amount } where the total across all entries equals the sum of all selected batch amounts. Boxes in a folder are randomly selected (min 5 boxes even if fewer assigned).
core_concepts.DISTRIBUTION: The full array of all generated Folder objects. Created in a single generation run. Must be reset before batches can be edited.
core_concepts.REPORT: The exportable output — either a plain-text summary or a structured PDF — listing all batch assignments and every folder's allocation details.

Batch.shape: { id: Number, amount: Number, boxNumbers: Number[] }
Batch.amount: Integer ₹ value assigned per box in this batch
Batch.boxNumbers: Sorted array of box numbers (1–100) this batch covers
Batch.constraint: A box number can only appear in ONE batch at a time. Cross-batch duplicates trigger a resolution dialog.

DistributionFolder.description: Plain object mapping box number strings to integer ₹ amounts
DistributionFolder.constraint: Sum of all values == amountPerFolder (total of all selected batch amounts). Min 5 box entries per folder regardless of assigned box count.

DuplicateModal.description: Transient state shown when a box being added already exists in another batch. Lets user choose 'Remove from other batch' or 'Proceed anyway'.

Notification.description: Ephemeral toast notification state — auto-dismissed by ErrorNotification component.

ConfirmDialogState.shape: { isOpen, title, message, confirmText, cancelText, onConfirm, onCancel, type }
ConfirmDialogState.description: Generic dialog config. Type drives icon/color: warning=amber, error=red, info=blue, success=green.

state_map.owner: AmountDistributionSimulator.jsx — the single source of truth for ALL app state

states.distributions: DistributionFolder[] — generated folder results (empty until generation)
states.numFolders: Number — how many folders to generate
states.editingId: Number|null — ID of batch currently being inline-edited
states.editAmount: Number|string — draft amount value during edit
states.editBoxes: string — draft comma-separated box numbers during edit
states.selectedBatches: Number[] — batch IDs selected for generation when >1 batch exists
states.showGridModal: Boolean — shows the 10x10 box grid modal
states.pendingBatchData: { amount, boxNumbers }|null — batch held during loading animation
states.confirmDialog: ConfirmDialogState|null — current confirm dialog (defined but reset uses window.confirm directly)

derived.boxMap: getAllBoxes() → { [boxNum]: amount } — flat map of all assigned boxes across all batches
derived.boxes: Array(100) — index-based array where index+1 = box number, value = amount or null
derived.assignedBoxes: Count of boxes with non-null values
derived.totalAmount: Sum of (amount × boxCount) across all batches
derived.amountPerFolder: Same formula as totalAmount — used as the target sum per generated folder

steps: 1. Filter batches by selected batchIds, collect all box numbers (flatMap).
2. Compute totalBoxCount and amountPerFolder (sum of amount × boxCount per batch).
3. effectiveBoxCount = Math.max(totalBoxCount, 5)  → always use at least 5 boxes per folder.
a. Pick effectiveBoxCount random unique box numbers from 1–100 (Fisher-Yates shuffle on full 100-array, slice first N).
b. Distribute amountPerFolder across those boxes:
- For each box except the last: pick randomAmount in range [1, max(1, remaining - remainingBoxes + 1)].
- Last box receives the exact remainder to guarantee folder total = amountPerFolder.
c. Build distribution object: { boxNum: amount }.
5. Set distributions state with the full array of folder objects.
distribution_algorithm.key_constraint: Each folder's box amounts must sum to exactly amountPerFolder. Guaranteed by assigning remainder to last box.
distribution_algorithm.randomness: Fisher-Yates shuffle. Math.floor(Math.random() * range) for individual amounts.

nodes.description: Bootstraps React 19 with StrictMode. Mounts <App /> into #root. Imports global index.css.
Renders the page skeleton: <Header /> → <main> → <AmountDistributionSimulator /> → <Footer />. Sets data-theme='light' on root div.
Sticky top bar with auto-hide on scroll-down (triggers after 80px scroll). Shows brand logo, title, and an info button that opens a How-To modal overlay explaining the 5-step usage flow.

Owns ALL state. Contains the distribution algorithm, export logic (TXT + PDF via jsPDF), batch CRUD, duplicate resolution, and inline edit. Renders: hero header, grid-toggle bar, InputSection, batch list, stats cards, folder-generation controls, ResultsPanel, download controls, and all modal/overlay components.

key_handlers.handleAssignAmount: Validates input and checks for box duplicates before triggering LoadingPopup
key_handlers.handleLoadingComplete: Fires after 5s animation — commits pendingBatchData to batches state
key_handlers.handleGenerateDistribution: If >1 batch exists shows BatchSelector modal, else calls generateDistributionsFromBatches directly
key_handlers.handleDuplicateResolve: Resolves box conflicts: 'remove' strips box from existing batch, 'proceed' ignores conflict
key_handlers.handleDeleteBatch / handleStartEdit / ha: Batch CRUD — all blocked if distributions already exist (must reset first)
key_handlers.handleDownload: Generates TXT blob or jsPDF document based on downloadFormat state

nodes.description: Purely presentational form. Two fields: Amount (number, prefix ₹) and Box Numbers (text, comma-separated). Parses box input to integer array, validates non-empty and positive amount locally, then calls onAssignAmount(amount, boxNumbers) prop. Supports Enter key submission. Clears fields after assign.

Pure display component. Receives distributions array and renders each folder as a card showing: folder index, total ₹ badge, and all box→amount allocations sorted by box number. Uses CSS animation-delay per card for staggered entrance.

Modal shell that wraps BoxGrid. Manages body scroll-lock (modal-open class on document.body) and a 250ms closing animation. Shows assigned box count in header. Accepts boxes array (100 items) and renders BoxGrid inside.
Renders all 100 boxes as a CSS grid. Each cell styled 'assigned' (highlighted, shows ₹ amount and dot) or 'empty'. Shows a legend at the bottom. Pure display — no event handlers.
nodes.props_in: boxes: (Number|null)[]  — index = boxNum-1, value = ₹ amount or null

nodes.description: Full-screen overlay shown during batch assignment. Runs a 5-step progress animation (Validating → Generating → Assigning → Updating → Complete) over exactly 5000ms using setInterval at 50ms ticks. On completion calls onComplete() after 400ms delay. Blocks UI during animation.
Displays ephemeral error/warning/success messages. Auto-dismisses (managed inside component via useEffect/timer). Receives notification state from parent.
nodes.props_in: notification: { message: string, type: string } | null

nodes.description: Generic modal with title, message, confirm and cancel buttons. Type prop ('warning'|'error'|'info'|'success') controls icon emoji and background color. Has 250ms closing animation. Fully controlled via props.
nodes.props_in: isOpen, title, message, confirmText, cancelText, onConfirm, onCancel, type

nodes.description: 3-column static info footer: About blurb, Features list, Quick Tips list. Bottom bar shows 'Deepak Distribution Systems' credit and Version 3.0.

edges.trigger: onAssignAmount fires on Assign click or Enter key

step_1_assign.label: Assign Amount to Boxes
step_1_assign.user_action: Enter ₹ amount + comma-separated box numbers → click 'Assign Amount'
step_1_assign.system_response: Validates input → checks for duplicate boxes → if valid, stores in pendingBatchData → triggers LoadingPopup (5-second animation) → on complete, commits new Batch to state

step_2_manage_batches.label: Review & Edit Batches
step_2_manage_batches.user_action: View batch list with amounts and box assignments. Can edit (inline) or delete any batch.
step_2_manage_batches.system_response: Edit/delete blocked if distributions already exist. Inline edit validates box uniqueness across all other batches.

step_3_view_grid.label: Inspect Box Grid (optional)
step_3_view_grid.user_action: Click 'View Box Grid' button (shows assigned count badge)
step_3_view_grid.system_response: Opens GridModal with 10×10 BoxGrid. Assigned boxes highlighted with amount. Empty boxes shown dimly.

step_4_generate.label: Generate Distribution Folders
step_4_generate.user_action: Enter number of folders → click 'Generate Distribution'
step_4_generate.system_response: If >1 batch: shows BatchSelector to choose which batches to include. Then runs algorithm → populates distributions array → ResultsPanel renders all folders.

step_5_export.label: Download Report
step_5_export.user_action: Choose TXT or PDF format → click 'Download Results'
step_5_export.system_response: TXT: generates plain-text report as Blob download. PDF: uses jsPDF to render a structured multi-page report with color-coded section headers, box assignment table, folder breakdowns, and grand total banner.

step_6_reset.label: Reset Everything
step_6_reset.user_action: Click 'Reset All' → confirm via window.confirm dialog
step_6_reset.system_response: Clears batches, distributions, numFolders, selectedBatches, showBatchSelector.

key_constraints_and_rules: A box number (1–100) can only be in ONE batch at a time. Duplicates trigger DuplicateModal.
Batches cannot be created, edited, or deleted once distributions have been generated — user must Reset first.
Each generated folder ALWAYS contains exactly N box entries where N = max(assignedBoxes, 5).
Each folder total ALWAYS equals amountPerFolder (guaranteed by assigning remainder to last box).
Box numbers in folders are randomly picked from the full 1–100 range (not just assigned boxes).
LoadingPopup always takes exactly 5 seconds regardless of actual computation time — it's a UX animation, not a real progress indicator.
Export (TXT/PDF) is only available when distributions exist (distributions.length > 0).
Header auto-hides on scroll-down past 80px and reappears on scroll-up.

file_index.src/index.css: Global CSS reset and base variables
file_index.src/components/AmountDistributionSimulat: Core engine + state owner + render orchestrator
file_index.src/components/Header.jsx: Sticky auto-hide nav bar + How-To info modal
file_index.src/styles/*.css: One CSS file per component — no shared utility classes
file_index.package.json: Dependencies: react@19, react-dom@19, jspdf@4. DevDeps: vite@8, eslint
file_index.index.html: HTML shell — single div#root mount point
file_index.dist/: Production build output (generated by npm run build)
